freshness_caveat: Coal (166 days old, as of 2025-12-26) and Clean Dark (161 days old, as of 2025-12-31) are stale; forward-looking scenarios rely on current news flow (Hormuz, sanctions) rather than recent coal/spread mechanics. TTF, DE Power, storage, and renewables current as of 2026-06-09/10.
risk_flags: Storage at 42.8% (19th percentile, 14.3 pp below seasonal) — refill pace into summer will set H2 power and gas risk if supply tightens further.
Coal at 96 USD/t (7th percentile) — thermal dispatch suppressed, but Hormuz closure and sanctions package 21 could elevate crude and marginal generation costs sharply.
Hormuz closure (Feb 28) spiked jet fuel; persistent shipping disruption raising LNG delivery premiums, tightening EU gas arbitrage and supporting TTF.
Russian sanctions package 21 freezes oil price cap — unclear net LNG export boost vs. supply-chain disruption; monitor implementation details.

base.summary: TTF holds 48–50 EUR/MWh; DE Power 125–135 EUR/MWh; coal suppression persists; renewables offset gas call.

downside.summary: Hormuz reopens; shipping normalizes; global LNG supply eases; renewables remain strong; storage refill accelerates; TTF softens.

upside.summary: Hormuz closure persists; sanctions package 21 Russian oil curbs lock in; crude spikes; LNG delivery costs rise; TTF and power rally.

themes: Storage deficit + coal depression = thermal generation headroom constrained
Geopolitical crude/LNG tightening via Hormuz and Russian sanctions
Renewables at 77th-percentile offsetting gas call, but storage lag creates H2 refill risk
Shipping fragmentation raising LNG delivery costs to EU
top_drivers: Storage deficit (42.8%, 14.3 pp below seasonal) + coal suppression (7th percentile) = tight thermal backstop into summer, vulnerable to supply shock.
Geopolitical crude tightening (Hormuz closure, Russian sanctions package 21) pushes Brent higher and feeds into EU power generation cost curve via fuel-switch and LNG arbitrage mechanics.
Shipping route fragmentation and elevated LNG delivery premiums narrow EU-Asia arbitrage, supporting TTF and constraining EU import optionality.
top_takeaway: EU storage 14 pp below seasonal; coal at 7th-percentile; Hormuz closure and Russian sanctions tighten crude-to-power cost chain via LNG arbitrage and fuel-switch exposure.

watchlist.why: Closure spiked crude and jet fuel; persistent disruption raises global LNG delivery costs and supports TTF via arbitrage tightening.

watchlist.metric: EU sanctions package 21 implementation (Russian oil export curbs)
watchlist.why: Tighter Russian crude export restrictions reduce global supply, pushing Brent higher and elevating EU power generation costs via fuel-switch exposure.

watchlist.metric: EU storage refill pace (seasonal +14.3 pp deficit)
watchlist.why: Slow intra-year refill (currently +16.5% m/m but from low base) — if supply tightens or weather turns hot, H2 storage stress and power curve inversion risk.
Fragmented routes and higher transit costs narrow EU-Asia LNG arbitrage, support TTF and reduce European import competitiveness vs. Atlantic basin.

watchlist.metric: North African solar project deployment milestones
watchlist.why: Long-term structural bearish for EU power baseload; €5B investment under way but timeline uncertain — monitor grid interconnection announcements for 2027+ impact.

watchlist_dated.name: EU sanctions package 21 implementation details (Russian oil export curbs)
watchlist_dated.why: Clarify export volume curbs and timeline; direct input to crude-to-power cost pass-through and LNG re-routing risk.

watchlist_dated.name: Hormuz strait reopening or escalation update
watchlist_dated.why: Current closure (Feb 28) is live; any reopening or further closure spreads directly into LNG delivery costs and EU gas arbitrage.
